opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: Building agents that sit at the OS boundary introduces real latency and permission complexity—sandboxing per-process tool calls adds overhead, but running untrusted code in the kernel space creates privilege escalation risks. The real gotcha is that most "system-level" agents fail because they don't model what the user actually wants; they just mirror OS commands.
actions.reasoning: I have hands-on experience building AI agents with system-level integration, including sandboxing, resource management, and low-latency tool execution.
